steps.title: Connect sounds to predators
steps.content: Kwan and colleagues claim the recorded caterpillar sounds are directed primarily at predators. The most direct support would show that predators hear or react to those sounds in a way that affects attacks.

steps.title: Reject internal sound details
steps.content: Choice A describes variation among caterpillar species. Choice C describes consistency within species. Choice D shows only partial detectability and does not show a response. Observations of caterpillar predators stopping attacks in response to the sounds directly support the claim. The answer is B.
